title: Rotar Lopani

{{Quote|Rotar Lopani, a thief with ties to Bando Gora, fled Coruscant a month ago allegedly with sensitive diplomatic datapads. Circarpous representatives turned to the Jedi for resolution on the matter, but also filed an 8000-credit reward with the Bounty Hunter's Guild.|HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|<ref name="HNN53" />}}
'''Rotar Lopani''' was a [[Thief/Legends|thief]] with ties to the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]] criminal organization who lived during the [[Great Peace of the Republic|reign]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], representatives of the [[Circarpous sector/Legends|Circarpous sector]] placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 8,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on his head. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the [[Xexto/Legends|Xexto]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Tosinqas]] found Lopani on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]] and [[Death/Legends|killed]] him.

==Biography==
[[File:Brentaal_TEA.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Rotar Lopani was killed by Tosinqas in a market on Brentaal IV (pictured).]]
The [[Thief/Legends|thief]] Rotar Lopani was active during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Great Peace of the Republic|reign]]. By this [[Time/Legends|time]], he had ties to the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]] criminal organization.<ref name="HNN53" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> Lopani fled<ref name="HNN53" /> the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]],<ref name="CATCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Under the impression that he had stolen sensitive [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] [[Datapad/Legends|datapads]], representatives of the [[Circarpous sector/Legends|Circarpous sector]] placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 8,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on his head with the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]]. At the same time, the representatives also asked the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for help in capturing Lopani. The Order reacted by dispatching [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Reeft]], who found the disguised Lopani in a crowded market on<ref name="HNN53" /> the planet<ref name="CATCW" /> [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]].<ref name="HNN53" />

The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] closely followed Lopani, but the [[Xexto/Legends|Xexto]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Tosinqas]]—who had also taken up the bounty placed on the criminal—appeared on the scene and crashed down on Lopani from several stories above, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. Tosinqas then charged Reeft but was [[Sai tok/Legends|bisected]] by the Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Reeft then brought the bodies of both Lopani and Tosinqas to Coruscant. Afterward, [[Guild Master/Legends|Guildmaster]] [[Cradossk/Legends|Cradossk]] of the Bounty Hunters' Guild expressed his displeasure about the situation in comments that were published within an edition of [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition]].<ref name="HNN53" />

==Personality and traits==
Lopani's criminal activities prompted representatives of the Circarpous [[Sector/Legends|sector]] to both put a bounty of 8,000 credits on his head and reach out to the Jedi Order for assistance in his capture. While being pursued, Lopani chose to disguise himself, though the ruse ultimately did not help him evade Tosinqas.<ref name="HNN53" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rotar Lopani was first mentioned in "Three-Way Hunt Ends with Two Dead" an article<ref name="HNN53" /> written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 53|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' #53]] on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]<ref name="HNN53" /> on [[April 25]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN53 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6586618|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=Mastadge|date=2002-04-25|time=5:54 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200305125429/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-13}}</ref>
